name: La era de San Bernabé Trail
name_original: Subida al Mirador Era de San Bernabé — circular desde Casas del Castañar
description: Jog the difficult 6.2-mile La era de San Bernabé Trail in Valle del Jerte, climbing 1051 feet to panoramic views from the Era de San Bernabé
region_intro: Jogging routes around Casas Del Castañar traverse a landscape characterized by river valleys, water features like reservoirs and waterfalls, and surrounding hills. The region offers varied terrain suitable for running, from riverside paths to more challenging trails with elevation changes. These routes often follow natural waterways and lead through areas with diverse vegetation. The elevation gains on some routes indicate a landscape that includes both flatter sections and inclines. Best jogging routes around Casas Del Castañar The most popular jogging route is Puente de San Lázaro by the Paseo Fluvial Plasencia, Aguas Abajo — circular by the Pantano de Plasencia, a 9.5 miles (15.3 km) trail that takes 1 hour 36 minutes to complete. This difficult circular route follows the river and reservoir. Another top favourite among local runners is Plasencia Riverwalk Trail, a moderate 7.5 miles (12.1 km) path. This trail follows the river with varied natural surroundings. Local runners also love the Cascada del Caozo Trail, a 5.1 miles (8.3 km) trail leading through a waterfall and surrounding forest, often completed in about 1 hour 3 minutes. Jogging around Casas Del Castañar is defined by river paths, reservoir views, and trails leading to waterfalls, with options for both moderate and difficult running experiences. The network offers routes for various ability levels, from steady riverwalks to more challenging climbs. The routes in Casas Del Castañar are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 12 reviews. More than 400 runners have used komoot to explore Casas Del Castañar's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: Are there any running routes suitable for beginners in Casas Del Castañar?
A: While the listed routes are generally moderate to difficult, the region's landscape offers varied terrain. For a less strenuous experience, consider exploring flatter sections along river paths. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1295142952/plasencia-riverwalk-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plasencia Riverwalk Trail&lt;/a&gt;, though moderate, follows the river with varied natural surroundings and has manageable elevation changes, making it a good option for those looking for a steady run.

Q: What are some longer running trails available in the Casas Del Castañar area?
A: For a longer challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1295180443/puente-de-san-lazaro-by-the-paseo-fluvial-plasencia-aguas-abajo-circular-by-the-pantano-de-plasencia" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente de San Lázaro by the Paseo Fluvial Plasencia, Aguas Abajo — circular by the Pantano de Plasencia&lt;/a&gt; offers a substantial 9.5 miles (15.3 km) route, following the river and reservoir. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990929149/route-of-the-white-mojon-running-circular-from-piornal-pr-cc-14" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the White Mojón – running circular from Piornal (PR-CC 14)&lt;/a&gt;, which is 8.5 miles (13.8 km) long.

Q: Are there any circular jogging routes around Casas Del Castañar?
A: Yes, several routes are circular. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1295180443/puente-de-san-lazaro-by-the-paseo-fluvial-plasencia-aguas-abajo-circular-by-the-pantano-de-plasencia" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente de San Lázaro by the Paseo Fluvial Plasencia, Aguas Abajo — circular by the Pantano de Plasencia&lt;/a&gt; is a popular circular option. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990929149/route-of-the-white-mojon-running-circular-from-piornal-pr-cc-14" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the White Mojón – running circular from Piornal (PR-CC 14)&lt;/a&gt; is also a circular trail, offering a scenic loop.

Q: What kind of scenic views can I expect on the running trails in Casas Del Castañar?
A: The running routes in Casas Del Castañar are known for their beautiful natural scenery. You can expect river valleys, views of reservoirs like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5391554" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de Plasencia&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6090688" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plasencia Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, and trails leading to impressive waterfalls. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990926132/cascada-del-caozo-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cascada del Caozo Trail&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, takes you through a forest to a waterfall.

Q: Are there any waterfalls to see along the jogging routes?
A: Absolutely! The region is rich in water features. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990926132/cascada-del-caozo-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cascada del Caozo Trail&lt;/a&gt; specifically leads through a waterfall and surrounding forest. Other notable waterfalls in the area include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4204503" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nogaleas Waterfalls&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4360769" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Caozo Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, which are popular highlights.

Q: What do other runners say about the jogging experience in Casas Del Castañar?
A: The komoot community highly rates the running trails in Casas Del Castañar, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 12 reviews. Runners often praise the varied terrain, from steady riverwalks to more challenging climbs, and the beautiful natural surroundings including river paths and reservoir views.

Q: Are there any challenging running routes for experienced runners?
A: Yes, for experienced runners seeking a challenge, there are several difficult routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1295180443/puente-de-san-lazaro-by-the-paseo-fluvial-plasencia-aguas-abajo-circular-by-the-pantano-de-plasencia" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente de San Lázaro by the Paseo Fluvial Plasencia, Aguas Abajo — circular by the Pantano de Plasencia&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 9.5-mile route with notable elevation changes. Another challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1296805802/la-era-de-san-bernabe-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La era de San Bernabé Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which is 6.2 miles (10 km) long and features significant ascents.

Q: Are there any natural monuments or points of interest near the running trails?
A: Beyond the waterfalls, the area around Casas Del Castañar features several natural monuments. You can find highlights like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5638455" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Natural pools of Valdastillas&lt;/a&gt;, which offer a refreshing stop. The region's river valleys and reservoirs themselves are significant natural attractions.

Q: How many running routes are available in Casas Del Castañar?
A: There are over 15 dedicated running routes around Casas Del Castañar listed on komoot, offering a diverse range of experiences from moderate riverwalks to more challenging trails with significant elevation changes.

Q: What is the best time of year for jogging in Casas Del Castañar?
A: The region's climate, characterized by river valleys and surrounding hills, makes spring and autumn ideal for jogging, offering milder temperatures. Summers can be warm, so early mornings or late evenings are preferable, especially on routes with less shade. Winters are generally mild, but some higher elevation trails might be cooler.

Q: Are there any running routes that are particularly suitable for families?
A: While specific 'family-friendly' designations aren't provided, routes with moderate difficulty and less extreme elevation changes would be most suitable. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1295142952/plasencia-riverwalk-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plasencia Riverwalk Trail&lt;/a&gt;, with its varied natural surroundings along the river, could be a good option for families looking for a steady and enjoyable run.

Q: Can I find running routes with cafes or places to stop for refreshments?
A: While specific cafes directly on the trails are not detailed, many routes are accessible from or pass near towns and villages where you can find establishments for refreshments. Planning your route to start or end in a village like Casas Del Castañar or Plasencia will give you access to local amenities.
tips: This curious iron structure offers fantastic views of the entire Jerte valley and the Plasencia reservoir. It has a covered part that houses a couple of loungers and a bench to relax before continuing the march.
Beautiful landscapes of fields of trees that in spring times are in full bloom in a beautiful atmosphere to walk through!

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is beautiful year-round, but spring (especially during cherry blossom season) and autumn offer particularly stunning scenery and pleasant temperatures for jogging. The dense oak and chestnut forests provide ample shade, making it enjoyable even on warmer days.

faqs.question: Is the La era de San Bernabé Trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: While generally considered 'easy' for hiking, this trail is rated as difficult for jogging due to sustained ascents and varied terrain. Beginners might find the elevation gain challenging, so a good level of physical condition is recommended for running it comfortably.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start, and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The trail conveniently starts and ends in the charming village of Casas del Castañar. You can begin your journey from a fountain in the village, where the cobbled path leads uphill. Parking is generally available within or on the outskirts of Casas del Castañar.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the route?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter a diverse mix of surfaces. The trail begins with a clearly marked, cobbled path, transitioning to paved and dirt roads. Some sections become unpaved, featuring uphill climbs and descents through forests and orchards.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail offers spectacular panoramic views, especially from the Era de San Bernabé Viewpoint. From here, you can admire the Jerte River valley, the Riscos de Villavieja, the Jerte reservoir, and even the city of Plasencia. You'll also pass through dense oak and chestnut forests and seasonal fruit trees of Casas del Castañar.

faqs.question: Is the trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the La era de San Bernabé Trail is generally dog-friendly. However, always ensure your dog is under control, preferably on a leash, to protect local wildlife and respect other trail users. Remember to carry water for your pet, especially on warmer days.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to access the trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to jog or hike the La era de San Bernabé Trail. It is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route is part of the PR-CC 24. In its initial sections, it also overlaps with the SL-CC 35 "Ruta de los Castaños" (Chestnut Tree Route), highlighting the region's rich natural heritage.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities or places to eat near the trailhead?
faqs.answer: Casas del Castañar, where the trail starts and ends, is a charming village that offers cafes, pubs, and accommodation options. It's a great place to refuel or relax after your jog.

faqs.question: What is the 'Era de San Bernabé' and why is it significant?
faqs.answer: The 'Era de San Bernabé' is an ancient circular threshing floor that now serves as a magnificent viewpoint. It's a key highlight of the trail, offering breathtaking vistas of the Jerte Valley and beyond. The viewpoint is well-equipped with seating and informative panels, making it an ideal spot to pause and appreciate the scenery.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a jog on this trail?
faqs.answer: Given the varied terrain and elevation changes, we recommend wearing appropriate trail running shoes. Bring sufficient water, especially during warmer months, and consider snacks. A hat and sunscreen are also advisable, though much of the route is shaded by forests.

waypoints.name: Fruit trees of Casas del Castañar
